title: Explicit heat grid total is preserved when the boundary term is zero

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the heat boundary term is zero, then the total of the shifted heat grid update equals the original interior total.
assumptions: r is a real diffusion number
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n is a natural-number range length
informal.expected_conclusion: The heat grid total is preserved under a zero boundary term.

declarations.name: heatInteriorTotal
declarations.statement: def heatInteriorTotal (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Real := Finset.sum (Finset.range n) (fun i => u (i + 1))

declarations.name: heatStepGridTotal
declarations.statement: def heatStepGridTotal (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Real := Finset.sum (Finset.range n) (fun i => heatStepGrid r u i)

declarations.name: heatBoundaryTerm
declarations.statement: def heatBoundaryTerm (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Real := u 0 - u 1 + (u (n + 1) - u n)

declarations.name: heatStepGridTotal_of_boundary_zero
declarations.statement: theorem heatStepGridTotal_of_boundary_zero (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hboundary : heatBoundaryTerm u n = 0) : heatStepGridTotal r u n = heatInteriorTotal u n

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation total-balance theorem into Lean 4: If the heat boundary term is zero, then the total of the shifted heat grid update equals the original interior total.
tasks.target: theorem heatStepGridTotal_of_boundary_zero (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hboundary : heatBoundaryTerm u n = 0) : heatStepGridTotal r u n = heatInteriorTotal u n

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by rewriting heatStepGridTotal_eq, using the zero-boundary hypothesis, and ring.

tasks.input: Find the heat total-balance theorem used to prove preservation under a zero boundary term.
